title: I18N-AUTO-TRANSLATE
scope: Phase 43 [I18N-DEPTH] shipped TranslationSuggestionService with a fromConfig() factory + a match-statement dispatch over driver=stub|google|deepl. Both google() and deepl() currently fall through to stub() — the real API wiring was deferred to Phase 52+. Phase 44 [I18N-RTL] registered Arabic as a first-class locale but ALL ar/*.php files still carry [TODO-ar] placeholders for hundreds of strings — every Arabic user sees English fallbacks for any string the human translator hasn't filled in. lang:suggest currently prints the suggestion as a copy-paste fragment; no --apply mode writes to lang files. No cost guard exists — a runaway translation job could rack up API spend without warning. Phase 52 closes all five gaps: (1) extracts a TranslationDriverInterface + Stub driver, (2) implements GoogleTranslationDriver using Laravel Http (no SDK dependency, mockable via Http::fake()), (3) implements DeepLTranslationDriver with formality+free/pro endpoint support, (4) gives lang:suggest --apply atomic write-with-backup behaviour, (5) adds i18n_translation_spend_usd gauge with sev3 $20/day budget refusal.

categories.DRIVER-INTERFACE: Extract App\Services\I18n\Contracts\TranslationDriverInterface (translate + costEstimateUsd). Move the existing stub() method into App\Services\I18n\Drivers\StubTranslationDriver. TranslationSuggestionService accepts a TranslationDriverInterface in its constructor + delegates suggest() to driver->translate(). New App\Services\I18n\TranslationDriverFactory::make(string $key) returns the right driver instance from config keys.
categories.GOOGLE-DRIVER: App\Services\I18n\Drivers\GoogleTranslationDriver implements TranslationDriverInterface using Laravel's Http facade (NOT the google-cloud-translate SDK — no composer change, Http::fake() makes it testable). Endpoint: https://translation.googleapis.com/language/translate/v2 with key= query param. Token-bucket rate limiter (300 req/min platform-wide via Cache::increment) refuses calls past quota with a graceful stub fallback. 4xx responses log a warning + fall back to stub so a single bad request doesn't kill the batch.
categories.DEEPL-DRIVER: App\Services\I18n\Drivers\DeepLTranslationDriver implements TranslationDriverInterface using Http facade. Picks endpoint based on API key suffix (free keys end in ':fx', pro keys do not). Supports formality option (formal/informal) for locales that accept it (de/fr/es/...). Glossary integration is a stub for now (config('i18n.deepl_glossary_id') passed through if present).
categories.APPLY-WORKFLOW: lang:suggest gains --apply flag that writes the suggested keys directly into lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php via a safe parse/merge/varexport flow. Before write, creates lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php.bak.<timestamp> snapshot so a botched run is reversible. New lang:review command lists every lang file row that contains the '[TODO:' marker so human translators can see the queue. App\Services\I18n\LangFileWriter::merge takes existing array + suggestions array + returns merged array (deep-merge, never overwrites existing strings).
categories.COST-GUARD: App\Services\I18n\TranslationCostTracker records every translate() call's characterCount + driver + locale into Cache::increment so we have a rolling 24h spend estimate. Service rejects further translations when daily budget exceeds config('i18n.daily_budget_usd', 20). Each driver's costEstimateUsd returns its per-char cost (Google ~$20/M chars, DeepL ~$25/M chars for pro). Sev3 alert row defines the contract; gauge wiring is the contract for ops.
categories.CI: Phase52I18nAutoTranslateSurfaceTest covers all 5 categories with Http::fake() to mock Google + DeepL responses + assert the cost tracker increments correctly + assert lang:suggest --apply writes the file + creates a .bak. Confirms TranslationDriverInterface exists. docs/runbooks/i18n.md gains an Auto-translate section: budget contract, fallback behaviour, glossary recipe, RTL post-translation review. alert-thresholds.md gains i18n_translation_spend_usd_24h sev3 row.

findings.title: Extract TranslationDriverInterface + StubTranslationDriver
findings.evidence: Current TranslationSuggestionService is monolithic — stub/google/deepl are three private methods. Adding real Google + DeepL implementations as private methods bloats the class beyond reasonable testability. Standard Laravel pattern is a driver interface + per-driver class.
findings.remediation: Create App\Services\I18n\Contracts\TranslationDriverInterface with translate(text, sourceLocale, targetLocale): string + costEstimateUsd(int characterCount): float. Create App\Services\I18n\Drivers\StubTranslationDriver returning '[TODO:<locale>] <text>' and costEstimateUsd 0.0. Move the implementation out of TranslationSuggestionService.

findings.title: TranslationSuggestionService delegates to injected driver
findings.evidence: After the interface exists, the service should hold a TranslationDriverInterface reference instead of switching on a driver string. This makes the service trivially mockable in unit tests (pass a fake driver) without Http::fake.
findings.remediation: TranslationSuggestionService constructor takes TranslationDriverInterface. suggest() delegates to driver->translate(). fromConfig() resolves driver via TranslationDriverFactory::make. Existing match statement removed.

findings.title: TranslationDriverFactory::make(string $driver)
findings.evidence: fromConfig() currently hardcodes the match. With three drivers + future glossary drivers + tests passing fakes, a factory centralises the resolution so callers stay 1-line.
findings.remediation: App\Services\I18n\TranslationDriverFactory::make(string $key): TranslationDriverInterface — returns Stub/Google/DeepL driver wrapped with TranslationCostTracker decorator if available. Unknown driver throws InvalidArgumentException.

findings.title: GoogleTranslationDriver — Http facade implementation
findings.evidence: Current google() method falls through to stub. Without a real implementation, Arabic + Swahili contributors copy-paste Stub output and hand-translate, slowing the i18n rollout. Implementing via Laravel Http facade (NOT the heavyweight google-cloud-translate SDK) keeps composer.json clean + tests use Http::fake().
findings.remediation: App\Services\I18n\Drivers\GoogleTranslationDriver uses Http::post('https://translation.googleapis.com/language/translate/v2', [...])->throw()->json(); parses data.translations[0].translatedText. API key + endpoint URL injected via constructor. costEstimateUsd returns characterCount * 20e-6 (Google's $20/M chars).

findings.title: Google driver — token-bucket rate limiter (300 req/min)
findings.evidence: Google's default API quota is 600 req/min/project; running lang:suggest --apply against 16 namespaces × 200 keys × 4 locales = 12800 requests would burn it in ~21 minutes. A token bucket throttling client-side prevents 429 noise.
findings.remediation: TranslationCostTracker (or a dedicated RateLimiter) uses Cache::increment with 60s window; refuses calls past 300 with a stub fallback + log warning. Cache key i18n:google:rate:{minute}.

findings.title: Google driver — error mapping to stub fallback + logged warning
findings.evidence: A malformed input or transient 5xx must not kill a 12800-row batch. Driver should catch + log + fall back to stub for the single failing row, returning '[TODO:locale] <text>' so the lang file still gets a row.
findings.remediation: Try/catch around the Http call. On any non-200, Log::warning with response status + first 200 chars of body; return stub string. Caller never sees an exception.

findings.title: DeepLTranslationDriver — Http facade implementation
findings.evidence: Phase 43 stubbed DeepL alongside Google. DeepL produces better translations for European languages + Arabic; landlords with Arabic tenants get measurably better UX with DeepL than Google. Free tier supports 500K chars/month free.
findings.remediation: App\Services\I18n\Drivers\DeepLTranslationDriver uses Http with Authorization: DeepL-Auth-Key header; endpoint chosen by suffix — keys ending in ':fx' = https://api-free.deepl.com, otherwise https://api.deepl.com. costEstimateUsd returns characterCount * 25e-6 (pro tier ~ $25/M chars).

findings.title: DeepL driver — formality option for supported locales
findings.evidence: DeepL supports formality (formal/informal) for several locales — for PropManager this matters most for German + French rental contracts where formal voice is contractually required. Even though we don't currently support de/fr, the option threads through so a Phase 53 locale addition just works.
findings.remediation: Driver constructor accepts ?string $formality; pushes formality=formal|informal field on the form payload when set + locale supports it (a small static map in the driver).

findings.title: DeepL driver — auto-detect free vs pro endpoint
findings.evidence: Free-tier DeepL keys end in ':fx' and require api-free.deepl.com; pro keys hit api.deepl.com. A single env var should work for both — auto-detection keeps the dev/prod config identical.
findings.remediation: Driver inspects api key suffix on construction; sets endpointBase accordingly. Documented in i18n.md.

findings.title: lang:suggest --apply atomic write into lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php
findings.evidence: Current lang:suggest prints to stdout — contributor copy-pastes into the lang file. This breaks for 200+ keys (manual copy-paste error rate too high) + slows the Arabic rollout. --apply automates the write while preserving existing translations.
findings.remediation: LangSuggest accepts --apply flag. With --apply: reads the lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php file (or empty array if absent), deep-merges the suggestions into it (NEVER overwrites existing values), writes via var_export, atomically via tmp file + rename. Without --apply: current stdout behaviour preserved.

findings.title: lang:review command — list every lang row carrying [TODO: marker
findings.evidence: After --apply, the lang files contain a mix of human translations + auto-translated rows (prefixed with the locale marker) + remaining stub rows ([TODO:<locale>]). Reviewers need a way to find the rows that still need human review. A grep across lang/ works but a command surfaces it consistently.
findings.remediation: App\Console\Commands\LangReview: scans lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php files; emits a table of (namespace, dotted-key, source value, current value) for every row matching /\[TODO:[a-z-]+\]/. Supports --locale and --namespace filters.

findings.title: Backup file before mutating lang/<locale>/<namespace>.php
findings.evidence: A botched --apply run could mangle hundreds of translations. A timestamped .bak snapshot of the previous file before write makes recovery trivial (cp the .bak over the .php).
findings.remediation: LangFileWriter::backup($path) copies $path to $path.bak.<timestamp> BEFORE overwriting. Backup is best-effort (logged warning if it fails, write proceeds) so an empty target file doesn't block the first run.

findings.title: TranslationCostTracker — rolling 24h spend gauge + budget refusal
findings.evidence: A runaway lang:suggest --apply (16 ns × 200 keys × 4 locales × 100 chars × $20e-6/char = ~$2.50/batch) is cheap individually but a buggy loop or repeated runs could rack up $100s of dollars in a day. Standard cost discipline (Phase 35 [PLATFORM]) demands a budget guard.
findings.remediation: App\Services\I18n\TranslationCostTracker::record(driver, locale, characterCount, perCharCostUsd) updates Cache::increment for daily spend + per-locale spend. ::canSpend(estimatedUsd) compares config('i18n.daily_budget_usd', 20) - currentSpend; returns false if over budget. Factory wraps every non-stub driver via a CostAwareDriver decorator that calls canSpend pre-call + record post-call.

findings.title: Per-locale cost attribution gauge
findings.evidence: Knowing the daily total isn't enough — ops want to see which locales are eating the budget. Per-locale gauge gives spend visibility.
findings.remediation: TranslationCostTracker records into both i18n:translation:spend:daily:total and i18n:translation:spend:daily:{locale}. Both surfaces are gauges; the per-locale rows are visibility-only (no threshold).

findings.title: alert-thresholds.md — i18n_translation_spend_usd_24h sev3 row
findings.evidence: Phase 35 [PLATFORM] alert pattern — every spend-bearing service has an alert-thresholds.md entry. Without the row defining the contract, the gauge isn't wired into the SRE escalation flow.
findings.remediation: alert-thresholds.md row: i18n_translation_spend_usd_24h sev3 threshold $20/day (matches config default). Source: TranslationCostTracker daily cache key. Action: review last 24h lang:suggest --apply runs; if legitimate, raise budget; if loop bug, kill the process + revert via .bak.

findings.title: Phase52I18nAutoTranslateSurfaceTest watchdog
findings.evidence: Phase 52 touches 5 new services + 1 new command + 2 lang-file mutators. Standard cycle pattern — one consolidated watchdog catches structural drift.
findings.remediation: tests/Feature/I18n/Phase52I18nAutoTranslateSurfaceTest with ~18 tests across all categories. Http::fake() to mock Google + DeepL responses. Asserts TranslationDriverInterface exists; GoogleTranslationDriver returns translatedText from fake response; cost tracker increments + refuses past budget; lang:suggest --apply writes file + creates .bak; lang:review surfaces TODO rows.

findings.title: docs/runbooks/i18n.md gains Auto-translate section
findings.evidence: The auto-translate workflow needs operator documentation: budget contract, fallback behaviour on quota exhaustion, when to use Google vs DeepL, RTL post-translation review tips. Without it, the next ops engineer to touch i18n re-discovers the conventions by reading code.
findings.remediation: i18n.md gains a Phase-52 Auto-translate section: 6 subsections (driver selection, API key setup, budget guard, --apply workflow, RTL review, incident playbook for runaway spend).

findings.title: Phase 43 → Phase 52 deferred-finding retrospective in i18n.md
findings.evidence: Phase 43 explicitly deferred 'real implementation deferred. Phase 44+ wires the actual API' in TranslationSuggestionService source. That comment is now stale + closing it documents the lineage from stub-scaffold to real-driver for future contributors.
findings.remediation: i18n.md gains a 'Phase 43 deferred-finding lineage' subsection — explains why Phase 43 shipped the stub-only driver pattern (workflow validation before API spend) + how Phase 52 closed it. TranslationSuggestionService source comment updated to remove the 'deferred' caveat.

DRIVER-INTERFACE-1.notes: App\Services\I18n\Contracts\TranslationDriverInterface with translate(text, source, target) + costEstimateUsd(int) + name() methods. App\Services\I18n\Drivers\StubTranslationDriver moved out of monolithic service; preserves [TODO:locale] marker shape so Phase 24 baseline ratchet is unaffected.

DRIVER-INTERFACE-2.notes: TranslationSuggestionService constructor now takes TranslationDriverInterface (defaults to StubTranslationDriver). suggest() delegates to driver->translate(). fromConfig() resolves driver via TranslationDriverFactory::make. Phase43I18nSurfaceTest (9 tests / 38 assertions) still passes unchanged.

DRIVER-INTERFACE-3.notes: App\Services\I18n\TranslationDriverFactory::make(string) returns Stub/Google/DeepL driver. Non-stub drivers wrapped with CostAwareDriver decorator when factory is constructed with a TranslationCostTracker. Unknown driver throws InvalidArgumentException — factory is the single resolution authority.

GOOGLE-DRIVER-1.notes: GoogleTranslationDriver uses Http::asForm()->post('translation.googleapis.com/language/translate/v2?key=...', [q, source, target, format=text]) — NO google-cloud-translate SDK dependency. Parses data.translations[0].translatedText. costEstimateUsd = chars * 20e-6 (Google's listed $20/M).

GOOGLE-DRIVER-2.notes: Driver constructor accepts maxPerMinute (default 300). Cache::increment with minute-window key i18n:google:rate:{minute}; refuses past cap with stub fallback. Watchdog test verifies the per-minute cap fires after N calls.

GOOGLE-DRIVER-3.notes: Try/catch around the Http call. On 4xx/5xx response or thrown exception: Log::warning with status + reason; return StubTranslationDriver()->translate(...). Caller never sees an exception. Tests verify both 4xx and 5xx paths fall through to [TODO:locale].

DEEPL-DRIVER-1.notes: DeepLTranslationDriver uses Http::withHeaders(['Authorization' => 'DeepL-Auth-Key {key}'])->post({endpoint}/v2/translate, [text, source_lang, target_lang]). Parses translations.0.text. costEstimateUsd = chars * 25e-6.

DEEPL-DRIVER-2.notes: Constructor accepts ?string $formality + ?string $glossaryId. formality field pushed onto payload ONLY when target locale in FORMALITY_LOCALES = [de, fr, es, it, nl, pl, pt, ja, ru]. glossary_id passed through when set.

DEEPL-DRIVER-3.notes: endpointBase(): keys ending in ':fx' return https://api-free.deepl.com; otherwise https://api.deepl.com. Watchdog tests confirm both branches via fake keys. Same I18N_DEEPL_API_KEY env var works for free + pro tiers.

APPLY-WORKFLOW-1.notes: LangFileWriter::merge(existing, suggestions) deep-merges additively — existing keys NEVER overwritten. ::write(path, payload) backs up to .bak.<timestamp> first, then writes via tmp file + atomic rename. var_export with custom indent (4-space) keeps lang files diff-friendly. lang:suggest gains --apply flag; without flag, prints to stdout as before.

APPLY-WORKFLOW-2.notes: App\Console\Commands\LangReview scans lang/*/*.php; emits a 4-col table (locale, namespace, key, value) for every row matching /\[TODO[:\-][a-zA-Z-]+\]/ (handles both Phase-52 colon marker + Phase-44 hyphen marker). --locale and --namespace filters. Smoke-tested against lang/ar/accounting → surfaced ~30 stub rows.

APPLY-WORKFLOW-3.notes: LangFileWriter::backup(path) copies path to path.bak.{unix-time}; logs warning + returns null on failure (best-effort, doesn't block write). Watchdog test_lang_file_writer_creates_backup_before_overwrite verifies the .bak.* glob.

COST-GUARD-1.notes: TranslationCostTracker: ::record(driver, locale, chars, costUsd) Cache::increments per-day total (in micro-USD to keep integer keys) + per-locale sibling; ::canSpend(estimatedUsd) compares against config('i18n.daily_budget_usd', 20.0). CostAwareDriver decorator runs canSpend pre-call + record post-call (only when result is not a [TODO: marker — failed calls don't charge).

COST-GUARD-2.notes: Tracker writes to both i18n:translation:spend:daily:total:{date} and i18n:translation:spend:daily:locale:{locale}:{date}. ::localeDailySpend(locale) returns the per-locale total. Alert-thresholds row is visibility-only (no threshold).

COST-GUARD-3.notes: alert-thresholds.md gained i18n_translation_spend_usd_24h sev3 row (threshold $20/day default, configurable via I18N_DAILY_BUDGET_USD env). Action: review last 24h lang:suggest --apply runs; legitimate spike → raise I18N_DAILY_BUDGET_USD; loop bug → kill process + revert via .bak.<timestamp> snapshots.

CI-1.notes: tests/Feature/I18n/Phase52I18nAutoTranslateSurfaceTest with 23 tests / 38 assertions. Http::fake mocks Google + DeepL responses. Verifies: driver interface + stub marker + factory + service delegation + Google parses translatedText + Google rate-limit fallback + Google 4xx/missing-key fallback + DeepL pro/free endpoint auto-detect + DeepL 5xx fallback + LangFileWriter merge invariant + .bak creation + lang:review registered + --apply flag in signature + cost tracker record + canSpend false past budget + CostAwareDriver fallback past budget + alert row presence + runbook section presence. All 23 pass.

CI-2.notes: docs/runbooks/i18n.md gains Phase-52 Auto-translate section with 6 subsections: driver selection table (stub/google/deepl with per-char cost + when to use), API key setup with .env example, budget guard mechanics, --apply workflow recipe (with concrete commands), RTL post-translation review tips, runaway-spend incident playbook (5-step recovery).

CI-3.notes: i18n.md gains a Phase 43 → Phase 52 deferred-finding lineage subsection explaining why Phase 43 shipped the stub-only driver pattern (workflow validation before API spend) + how Phase 52 closed it. TranslationSuggestionService source docblock updated — removed "Real implementation deferred" caveat, added Phase-52 DRIVER-INTERFACE-2 retro note.
